text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE]
DATE OF RECEIPT: [DATE]
DATE REPORTED: [DATE]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Urology / Interventional Radiology]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Genitourinary Oncology / Interventional Radiology
------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN ID: SP-[ACCESSION NUMBER]

CLINICAL HISTORY:
63-year-old female with an 11.4 cm heterogeneous right renal mass identified on cross-sectional imaging, associated with right renal vein invasion and infrarenal IVC thrombus (cT3bN2M1). Bilateral pulmonary nodules present. CT-guided core needle biopsy performed for tissue diagnosis prior to treatment planning.

TYPE OF SPECIMEN:
CT-guided core needle biopsy, right renal mass (percutaneous, 18-gauge, coaxial technique; 3 cores submitted)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

RIGHT RENAL MASS, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
   RENAL CELL CARCINOMA, CLEAR CELL TYPE (ccRCC)
   - WHO/ISUP Nuclear Grade: 3 (with focal areas of Grade 4 morphology
     in the sarcomatoid component; see comment)
   - Sarcomatoid differentiation: PRESENT, comprising approximately 20%
     of sampled tumor tissue
   - Rhabdoid differentiation: NOT IDENTIFIED
   - Tumor necrosis: Present (geographic, involving approximately 30-35%
     of core biopsy surface area)
   - Lymphovascular invasion: Cannot be assessed on core needle biopsy
     material
   - Margins: Not applicable (needle biopsy)

See ancillary studies below.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

The biopsy cores contain a biphasic neoplasm. The predominant component (approximately 80% of tumor area) shows classic clear cell RCC morphology: nests and alveoli of cells with abundant clear to finely granular cytoplasm, distinct cell membranes, and round to ovoid nuclei with prominent nucleoli visible at 10x magnification, consistent with WHO/ISUP nuclear grade 3. The intervening vasculature is delicate and arborizing, characteristic of clear cell RCC.

A sarcomatoid component is identified (approximately 20% of sampled area) and is composed of elongated spindle cells arranged in intersecting fascicles with markedly enlarged, pleomorphic nuclei, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and brisk mitotic activity (12 mitoses per 10 high-power fields in this component). Atypical mitoses are present. No rhabdoid cells (with eccentric nuclei and hyaline cytoplasmic inclusions) are identified in the material examined. Extensive geographic necrosis is present within both components.

The sarcomatoid morphology in the context of this renal primary is consistent with sarcomatoid differentiation of ccRCC (a recognized high-grade transformation), not a collision tumor or primary sarcoma. Immunohistochemistry supports this interpretation (see below).

Note: The degree of sarcomatoid differentiation estimated in this core biopsy (approximately 20%) may not be fully representative of the entire tumor given the inherently sampled nature of needle biopsy specimens.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and medical record number, is a container with three tan-white cylindrical core biopsy tissue fragments. Core 1 measures 1.5 cm in length x 0.15 cm in diameter. Core 2 measures 1.3 cm in length x 0.15 cm in diameter. Core 3 measures 1.2 cm in length x 0.15 cm in diameter. All three cores are entirely submitted. The tissue has a tan-gray, focally yellow appearance; areas of soft, necrotic consistency are appreciated grossly. All three cores are submitted entirely in one cassette, labeled A1.

Cassette Summary:
   A1 - all three core biopsy fragments, entirely submitted

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC)
Performed on paraffin-embedded tissue, block A1. Results interpreted in the context of the H&E morphology.

   PAX8:                   POSITIVE (diffuse, strong nuclear staining in
                           both clear cell and sarcomatoid components)
   CA9 (Carbonic Anhydrase IX): POSITIVE (diffuse membranous pattern,
                           strong; involving >95% of clear cell areas;
                           markedly reduced/focal in sarcomatoid areas)
   CD10:                   POSITIVE (diffuse membranous/cytoplasmic)
   Vimentin:               POSITIVE (diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining
                           in sarcomatoid component; focal in clear cell
                           areas)
   CK7:                    FOCALLY POSITIVE (patchy, weak cytoplasmic
                           staining; predominantly in clear cell
                           component)
   CK AE1/AE3 (pan-keratin): POSITIVE (diffuse in clear cell component;
                           focal in sarcomatoid component, consistent
                           with partial epithelial loss)
   SMA (smooth muscle actin): NEGATIVE in clear cell component;
                           FOCAL WEAK POSITIVE in sarcomatoid component
                           (non-specific)
   S100:                   NEGATIVE
   SOX10:                  NEGATIVE
   Desmin:                 NEGATIVE
   TFE3:                   NEGATIVE (no nuclear expression)
   PBRM1:                  LOSS OF EXPRESSION (absent nuclear staining
                           in tumor cells; retained in intratumoral
                           stromal/endothelial cells, serving as internal
                           positive control) -- see comment below
   BAP1:                   RETAINED EXPRESSION (intact nuclear staining
                           in tumor cells)
   Ki-67 (MIB-1):          Proliferation index:
                              - Clear cell component: approximately 18%
                              - Sarcomatoid component: approximately 35%

PD-L1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
   Antibody clone: 22C3 (Dako/Agilent platform)
   Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 40%
      (40% of viable tumor cells demonstrate membranous and/or cytoplasmic
      PD-L1 staining of any intensity)
   Combined Positive Score (CPS): Estimated 55 (high)
      (includes PD-L1 staining in tumor cells, tumor-associated
      macrophages, and lymphocytes; CPS = [PD-L1 stained cells /
      total viable tumor cells] x 100)
   Interpretation: HIGH PD-L1 EXPRESSION by CPS scoring.
   Note: PD-L1 expression is notably enriched in the sarcomatoid
   component and at the tumor-immune interface. PD-L1 IHC results are
   intended for research/therapeutic decision-making purposes and
   should be interpreted in the clinical context.

COMMENT ON PBRM1 LOSS:
Loss of PBRM1 protein expression by IHC is suggestive of an inactivating somatic mutation or deletion in the PBRM1 gene, a frequently altered tumor suppressor in ccRCC (encoding the BAF180 chromatin remodeling subunit). This finding is reported for informational purposes and may inform molecular profiling and clinical trial eligibility. Molecular confirmation is recommended (see note regarding reflex NGS below).

MOLECULAR TESTING:
Comprehensive genomic profiling (CGP) by next-generation sequencing (324-gene panel) has been ordered on residual tissue from this specimen. Results will be reported separately under a separate accession number when finalized. See pending NGS report.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Microscopic examination of H&E-stained sections demonstrates three biopsy cores. The majority of sampled tissue contains a cellular neoplasm with the following features:

Clear Cell Component (~80%): Tumor cells are arranged in compact nests, alveoli, and focally tubular structures supported by a rich network of thin-walled, sinusoidal vascular channels. Tumor cells have well-defined cell borders, abundant optically clear to finely granular pale cytoplasm (reflecting intracytoplasmic glycogen and lipid content, consistent with clear cell morphology), and round to ovoid nuclei. Nucleoli are clearly visible at 10x objective (WHO/ISUP grade 3), and occasional very large nuclei with macronucleoli are encountered at the border of the sarcomatoid component (WHO/ISUP grade 4 foci). Mitotic figures in the clear cell component: 4 per 10 HPF.

Sarcomatoid Component (~20%): Sheets and fascicles of large, spindle-shaped cells with scant amphophilic cytoplasm, markedly irregular nuclear contours, coarsely clumped chromatin, and prominent nucleoli. Numerous mitotic figures including atypical forms are identified (12/10 HPF in this component). Focal areas of necrosis are intimately associated with the sarcomatoid component.

Tumor Necrosis: Extensive geographic necrosis with karyorrhectic debris and ghost tumor cell outlines is present.

Stromal and Inflammatory Elements: The stroma contains scattered lymphocytes and macrophages, more prominent at the interface of viable and necrotic tumor. No granulomas. No amyloid.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY / SYNOPTIC REPORTING:
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Site:                      Right kidney (core needle biopsy)
Histologic type:           Renal cell carcinoma, clear cell type
WHO/ISUP nuclear grade:    3 (with grade 4 foci in sarcomatoid areas)
Sarcomatoid differentiation: Present (~20%)
Rhabdoid differentiation:  Absent
Tumor necrosis:            Present
LVI:                       Cannot be assessed
Margins:                   Not applicable
PD-L1 (22C3 CPS):         High (~55)
PBRM1 by IHC:              Loss of expression
BAP1 by IHC:               Retained
Ki-67:                     18% (clear cell); 35% (sarcomatoid)
NGS:                       PENDING (ordered separately)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST SIGN-OUT:
[Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Genitourinary Pathology Division

Electronic signature applied.
------------------------------------------------------------------------
